cards.note: Published on PyPI with CI, MIT licensed. The witnesses below are a live run on a generated world from the shipped benchmark.

witnesses.alt: Declared ground-truth causal DAG of a generated regional-coffee-chain world: eight variables (weekend, loyalty discount, supplier lead time, foot traffic, queue length, drinks sold, daily margin, and a hidden barista-morale confounder), with directed causal edges and the hidden confounder drawn dashed.
witnesses.claim: The declared ground-truth causal DAG of a generated coffee-chain world, rendered by the package itself: eight variables across disturbance, controllable, observable and outcome roles, the hidden confounder dashed.
witnesses.source: causal-worlds · benchmark/v0.2/world_00 · rendered with viz.to_dot + graphviz

witnesses.headline: On a generated coffee-chain world (8 variables, 9 ground-truth edges, one hidden confounder, 2000 rows), an interventional grader recovers the graph at F1 0.88 and keeps zero spurious confounded edges; observational PC reaches F1 0.84 but keeps the hidden-confounded pair as a causal edge.
witnesses.note: The gap is the point: correlation and observational PC mistake the hidden-confounded pair (queue length ~ supplier lead time) for a direct causal edge; only the interventional grader rejects it. Graded live with grade_spec against the declared answer key.

cards.title: causal-worlds
cards.author: Amit Timalsina
cards.direction: Turn a plain-language description of an operation into a fictional but coherent causal world with a declared ground-truth causal graph, then benchmark whether a causal-discovery method can recover it. Because the structure is declared, not learned, every generated world is an answer key: run any discovery method on the data and score how well it recovered the world.
